topic: Two coworkers discover they went to the same summer camp
raw_output:
[story 1]

The shared glance across the crowded breakroom was electric. Sarah felt a strange lightness bubble up in her chest, a sensation like champagne fizzing just beneath her ribs. When Mark finally looked up, his eyes widened, and a slow, utterly radiant smile spread across his face. "No way," he breathed, his voice thick with disbelief. Sarah couldn't help but laugh, a sound that felt surprisingly bright and unrestrained. The memory of sticky lake water and campfire songs flooded her mind, a wave of pure, unadulterated joy washing over her. She leaned against the counter, her whole body vibrating with an almost giddy energy. "You remember the canoe trip?" she managed, her voice a little breathless. Mark just shook his head, his grin softening into something deeply contented. "We were there. Together."

[story 2]

I nearly dropped my coffee mug when I saw his face. Liam’s expression shifted from mild surprise to something utterly luminous. A warmth, intense and spreading, began to bloom in my core, making my limbs feel buoyant. "Seriously?" I whispered, my voice sounding strangely high-pitched. I took a step closer, my gaze locked on his, unable to look away. The realization hit me—the shared secret of that forgotten summer—and a dizzying sense of homecoming washed over me. I started tapping my fingers rapidly on the desk, a restless, happy rhythm. "The archery competition," I said, a genuine, wide smile stretching my lips. Liam’s eyes crinkled at the corners, a deep, satisfied look settling in place. "I thought I was the only one who remembered that specific knot."

[story 3]

It was the sheer absurdity of the situation that made my cheeks flush. When Alex confirmed it, a feeling of soaring lightness took hold, lifting my shoulders. I couldn't stop smiling; it felt like my face was stretching into an expression of pure delight. I started pacing the small office space, my movements quick and light, as if I were floating on air. "We were both in Cabin Three," I exclaimed, my tone brimming with an almost breathless excitement. Alex watched me, his expression mirroring my own burgeoning happiness, a soft, pleased expression that made my heart do a little skip. "I thought that was just a rumor," he murmured, a genuine spark of shared history igniting between us. The memory of those weeks, the shared adventures, felt suddenly vibrant and perfect.
